target.name: Lumina Vehicles
target.founder: Ahmed Shubber (CEO; Fairfield U double-major mech + biomedical eng), Federico Di Palma (Co-founder)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ahmed Shubber (CEO, mechanical and biomedical engineering background) and Federico Di Palma. The company has raised $8M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 private robotics companies (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and 1 public comparable.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here. Using total raised ($8M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next stage yields $16M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector—while 7 same-sector peers exist broadly, filtering to similar-stage and similar-tier companies yields fewer than the 3 datapoints required for reliable median calculation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $18M, with a median of $16M. This range reflects the constraints of working from a single methodology and a limited peer set. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly matched stage and valuation-tier peers, which limits cross-validation, and potential sector-level volatility in robotics valuations. Any valuation decision should be informed by deeper due diligence on product development stage, customer traction, and unit economics relative to the identified peer set.
